decisions: Retained all daily logs (04-21 through 04-26): oldest (04-21) is 6 days old, still within 7-day window. Will become deletion candidate on 04-28.

No content promoted to any tier: reviewed 6 daily logs (memory/daily/2026-04-21.md through 2026-04-25.md plus TODAY.md) and found only heartbeat consolidation entries — zero user-generated content across all files.
observations: All four memory tiers (core, semantic, episodic, procedural) remain empty of user-generated content after 7 days — the entire capture pipeline is untested with real data.
Daily log chain is continuous and complete: every day with commits has a corresponding daily log file.
Consolidation self-assessments have passed trivially for 7 consecutive cycles because there is no real content to evaluate.
recommendations: On 04-28, delete memory/daily/2026-04-21.md (will be 7+ days old) — its single entry (bootstrap note) adds no lasting value beyond what episodic/reflection-2026-04-21.md already captures.
When the first real user session occurs, manually verify the full pipeline: daily log entry → promotion to appropriate tier → SUMMARY.md regeneration.
processImprovements: [self-critique] Seven days of consolidation with zero real content means I cannot assess whether my promotion logic works correctly. The self-assessment criteria all pass trivially, giving false confidence. I have no way to test this without actual user sessions.

[proposal] Consider extending reflection cadence to 5 days when the brain is idle (no user sessions in 7+ days). Three reflections so far have produced no new insights beyond 'still idle'. Rationale: reduces maintenance noise without losing coverage once activity resumes.
[blocked] Cannot test the full memory pipeline (capture → log → promote → summarize) without a real user session. This is an operational reality, not a process deficiency.
